body:
This refactoring PR shuffles code so that ~~all~~ more things related to distributed communications live in the DistributedComputations module. A few other important changes:

* I renamed `boundary_buffers` to `communication_buffers`. As far as I can tell, the purpose of these objects is to perform communication in distributed or multi-region situations.
* Now, `communication_buffers` are only constructed by the special `Field` constructor in `DistributedComputations`. Otherwise they are `nothing`.
* I restricted the construction of buffers to distributed grids. This fixes a bug wherein fields with distributed boundary conditions could not be constructed on serial CPU for post processing (as noted on https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/discussions/4199)
title: Don't invoke comm buffers on non-distributed grids and move to DistributedComputations

body:
Which can be triggered on PRs by adding the `benchmark performance` label.
These tests at the moment run on Tartarus GPU number 1. We probably need a better GPU / a GPU we know is free to perform these tests.
